nodes.label: Session 07 - Experiment Tracking and Hyperparameter Optimization
nodes.norm_label: session 07 - experiment tracking and hyperparameter optimization

nodes.label: EBS Volumes and EC2 Key Pairs
nodes.norm_label: ebs volumes and ec2 key pairs

nodes.label: LitServe
nodes.rationale: Flexible AI model serving engine built on FastAPI with batching, streaming, and GPU autoscaling.

nodes.label: FastAPI
nodes.rationale: LitServe is built on FastAPI and avoids rebuilding a FastAPI server per model.

nodes.label: LitAPI
nodes.rationale: Defines setup, decode_request, predict, batch, unbatch, and encode_response serving lifecycle steps.

nodes.label: Request Batching
nodes.rationale: Groups requests up to max_batch_size to improve throughput and hardware utilization.

nodes.label: Streaming Responses
nodes.rationale: Listed as a LitServe model-serving feature.

nodes.label: GPU Autoscaling
nodes.rationale: LitServe feature that scales GPU-backed inference workloads.

nodes.label: vLLM
nodes.rationale: Presented as more optimized than LitServe for fast LLM serving because of custom kernels, KV caching, and LLM-specific optimizations.

nodes.label: Gradio
nodes.rationale: Used to build simple model UIs and share demos with minimal code.

nodes.label: Streamlit
nodes.rationale: Mentioned with Gradio as a simple model prototype interface tool.

nodes.label: gr.Interface
nodes.rationale: Core Gradio abstraction connecting a prediction function to inputs and outputs.

nodes.label: gradio_client
nodes.rationale: Used to call a Gradio app predict endpoint programmatically.

nodes.label: TIMM Image Classifier
nodes.rationale: Example Gradio app wraps a TIMM model with preprocessing and ImageNet labels.

nodes.label: Hugging Face Sharing
nodes.rationale: Cloud sharing is recommended for putting prototype UIs behind a URL.

nodes.label: AWS Lambda
nodes.rationale: Serverless compute platform for event-driven, stateless, automatically scaling functions.

nodes.label: Event-Driven Execution
nodes.rationale: Lambda functions are triggered by AWS service events or custom sources.

nodes.label: Stateless Functions
nodes.rationale: Lambda functions do not maintain persistent data between invocations.

nodes.label: Firecracker MicroVM
nodes.rationale: AWS virtualization technology created for Lambda.

nodes.label: Gradio on AWS Lambda
nodes.rationale: Hands-on deployment packages a Gradio cat-vs-dog classifier for Lambda.

nodes.label: TorchScript Model
nodes.rationale: Example uses torch.jit.load to load a traced CPU model for serverless inference.

nodes.label: TorchServe
nodes.rationale: Open-source PyTorch model-serving library for scalable production deployment.

nodes.label: MAR Model Archive
nodes.rationale: TorchServe packages model artifacts and metadata into .mar files.

nodes.label: Dynamic Batching
nodes.rationale: TorchServe adjusts batch sizes based on incoming requests to improve inference throughput.

nodes.label: Model Versioning
nodes.rationale: TorchServe manages multiple model versions for testing and rollback.

nodes.label: Multi-Model Deployment
nodes.rationale: TorchServe can deploy multiple models in a single serving instance.

nodes.label: TorchServe Metrics and Monitoring
nodes.rationale: TorchServe exposes model performance and resource metrics.

nodes.label: Stable Diffusion 3 Medium
nodes.rationale: Example target model for TorchServe deployment.

nodes.label: Minikube
nodes.rationale: Local single-node Kubernetes cluster for development and testing.

nodes.label: Kubernetes
nodes.rationale: Container orchestration platform introduced through Minikube and kubectl workflows.

nodes.label: kubectl
nodes.rationale: CLI configured by Minikube to operate on Kubernetes clusters and namespaces.

nodes.label: Kubernetes Dashboard
nodes.rationale: Minikube add-on for monitoring and managing cluster resources.

nodes.label: Container Runtime
nodes.rationale: Minikube supports Docker and other runtimes or drivers for local Kubernetes.

nodes.label: Managed Kubernetes Services
nodes.rationale: EKS, GKE, and AKS are recommended for production instead of Minikube.

nodes.label: FastAPI Model Server
nodes.source_file: Session 14_ Kubernetes - II_ Ingress, ConfigMap, Secrets, Volumes and HELM.pdf
nodes.rationale: Kubernetes deployment example serves a TIMM model through FastAPI.

nodes.label: Redis Cache
nodes.source_file: Session 14_ Kubernetes - II_ Ingress, ConfigMap, Secrets, Volumes and HELM.pdf
nodes.rationale: Model server reads Redis environment variables and creates a Redis connection pool.

nodes.label: Kubernetes Ingress
nodes.source_file: Session 14_ Kubernetes - II_ Ingress, ConfigMap, Secrets, Volumes and HELM.pdf
nodes.rationale: Session covers exposing Kubernetes services through Ingress.

nodes.label: ConfigMap
nodes.source_file: Session 14_ Kubernetes - II_ Ingress, ConfigMap, Secrets, Volumes and HELM.pdf
nodes.rationale: Session covers externalizing application configuration for Kubernetes workloads.

nodes.label: Kubernetes Secret
nodes.source_file: Session 14_ Kubernetes - II_ Ingress, ConfigMap, Secrets, Volumes and HELM.pdf
nodes.rationale: Session covers storing sensitive configuration such as Redis password.

nodes.label: Kubernetes Volume
nodes.source_file: Session 14_ Kubernetes - II_ Ingress, ConfigMap, Secrets, Volumes and HELM.pdf
nodes.rationale: Session covers volumes as persistent or shared storage for pods.

nodes.label: Helm
nodes.source_file: Session 14_ Kubernetes - II_ Ingress, ConfigMap, Secrets, Volumes and HELM.pdf
nodes.rationale: Session covers Helm for templating and packaging Kubernetes manifests.

nodes.label: Amazon EKS
nodes.source_file: Session 15_ Kubernetes - III_ EKS, ALB, Auto Scaling.pdf
nodes.rationale: Fully managed Kubernetes control plane on AWS for production workloads.

nodes.label: Managed Kubernetes Control Plane
nodes.source_file: Session 15_ Kubernetes - III_ EKS, ALB, Auto Scaling.pdf
nodes.rationale: EKS provisions, scales, patches, and runs the Kubernetes control plane across availability zones.

nodes.label: Worker Nodes
nodes.source_file: Session 15_ Kubernetes - III_ EKS, ALB, Auto Scaling.pdf
nodes.rationale: Users create and manage worker EC2 instances that run containerized applications.

nodes.label: Application Load Balancer
nodes.source_file: Session 15_ Kubernetes - III_ EKS, ALB, Auto Scaling.pdf
nodes.rationale: EKS integrates with ALB and ELB for load balancing applications.

nodes.label: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler
nodes.source_file: Session 15_ Kubernetes - III_ EKS, ALB, Auto Scaling.pdf
nodes.rationale: EKS supports HPA for automatic scaling of application pods.

nodes.label: Cluster Autoscaler
nodes.source_file: Session 15_ Kubernetes - III_ EKS, ALB, Auto Scaling.pdf
nodes.rationale: EKS supports node scaling through Cluster Autoscaler.

nodes.label: AWS IAM
nodes.source_file: Session 15_ Kubernetes - III_ EKS, ALB, Auto Scaling.pdf
nodes.rationale: EKS integrates with IAM for authentication and fine-grained access control.

nodes.label: Amazon VPC Networking
nodes.source_file: Session 15_ Kubernetes - III_ EKS, ALB, Auto Scaling.pdf
nodes.rationale: EKS uses VPC networking, security groups, network ACLs, VPC peering, and PrivateLink.

nodes.label: IAM Roles for Service Accounts
nodes.source_file: Session 16 - Kubernetes - IV_ IRSA, Volumes, ISTIO & KServe.pdf
nodes.rationale: Maps Kubernetes service accounts to AWS IAM roles through OIDC for pod-level AWS access.

nodes.label: OIDC Provider
nodes.source_file: Session 16 - Kubernetes - IV_ IRSA, Volumes, ISTIO & KServe.pdf
nodes.rationale: EKS cluster must associate an IAM OIDC provider before creating IRSA service accounts.

nodes.label: S3 Service Account
nodes.source_file: Session 16 - Kubernetes - IV_ IRSA, Volumes, ISTIO & KServe.pdf
nodes.rationale: Example creates s3-list-sa with S3 permissions and attaches it to an AWS CLI pod.

nodes.label: EBS on EKS
nodes.source_file: Session 16 - Kubernetes - IV_ IRSA, Volumes, ISTIO & KServe.pdf
nodes.rationale: Session moves from IRSA to EBS storage on EKS.

nodes.label: Istio
nodes.source_file: Session 16 - Kubernetes - IV_ IRSA, Volumes, ISTIO & KServe.pdf
nodes.rationale: Service mesh component covered with KServe and volumes.

nodes.label: KServe
nodes.source_file: Session 16 - Kubernetes - IV_ IRSA, Volumes, ISTIO & KServe.pdf
nodes.rationale: Kubernetes-native model serving framework covered with Istio and EKS.

nodes.label: Canary Deployment
nodes.rationale: Deployment strategy explored through KServe, Knative revisions, and traffic rollout.

nodes.label: Knative Serving
nodes.rationale: Serverless layer in KServe that supports autoscaling, scale-to-zero, revision management, and canary rollout.

nodes.label: Scale to Zero
nodes.rationale: Knative/KServe serverless install supports scaling down to and from zero.

nodes.label: Revision Management
nodes.rationale: Knative supports versioned revisions used for canary rollout.

nodes.label: cert-manager
nodes.rationale: Installed as part of KServe with Knative and Istio setup.

nodes.label: KServe Serverless Installation
nodes.rationale: Enables request-volume autoscaling, scale-to-zero, revision management, and canary rollout.

nodes.label: Multi-Model Serving
nodes.rationale: Session deploys multiple classifier models and discusses autoscaling.

nodes.label: TorchServe Image Classification Handler
nodes.rationale: Single Transformers-based handler is reused across all image classification models.

nodes.label: Transformers ViT Image Models
nodes.rationale: Example handler uses ViTImageProcessor and AutoModelForImageClassification.

nodes.label: Hugging Face Classifier Models
nodes.rationale: Deploys ViT Base, food, cat breed, dog breed, and Indian food classifiers from Hugging Face.

nodes.label: Autoscaling for Multi-Model Deployment
nodes.rationale: Session title and deployment guidance focus on scaling multi-model inference.

nodes.label: Minikube-to-EKS Validation Path
nodes.rationale: Recommends testing KServe on single-node Minikube before deploying on EKS.

nodes.label: torch.compile
nodes.rationale: PyTorch 2.0 API that can speed up inference by compiling modules.

nodes.label: TorchDynamo
nodes.rationale: Captures PyTorch programs using Python frame evaluation hooks.

nodes.label: AOTAutograd
nodes.rationale: Generates ahead-of-time backward traces by tracing autograd.

nodes.label: PrimTorch
nodes.rationale: Canonicalizes PyTorch operators into a smaller primitive operator set.

nodes.label: TorchInductor
nodes.rationale: Compiler that generates fast code for accelerators and backends using Triton for NVIDIA and AMD GPUs.

nodes.label: OpenAI Triton
nodes.rationale: Key building block used by TorchInductor for GPU code generation.

nodes.label: CPU-GPU Orchestration Bottleneck
nodes.rationale: Optimization rationale explains that CPU Python orchestration can underfeed fast GPUs.

nodes.label: GGML
nodes.rationale: C library for machine learning primitives and binary LLM model distribution.

nodes.label: GGUF Format
nodes.rationale: Binary model format containing hyperparameters, vocabulary, and weights.

nodes.label: Integer Quantization
nodes.rationale: GGML supports 4-bit, 5-bit, and 8-bit quantization so large language models can run on consumer hardware.

nodes.label: llama.cpp
nodes.rationale: Course topic pairs GGML with llama.cpp for local LLM inference.

nodes.label: Consumer Hardware LLM Inference
nodes.rationale: Quantized GGML/GGUF models enable LLM inference outside large server GPUs.

nodes.label: Hardware-Specific CPU Optimization
nodes.rationale: GGML is optimized for Apple Silicon and uses AVX/AVX2 or VSX intrinsics on CPU architectures.

nodes.label: End-to-End MLOps Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Capstone requires a complete pipeline covering data versioning, training, optimization, containerization, and deployment.

nodes.label: DVC and S3 Data Versioning
nodes.rationale: Datasets must be versioned with DVC and stored in S3 for reproducibility and lineage.

nodes.label: Hyperparameter Optimization and Learning Rate Finder
nodes.rationale: Capstone requires HPO and learning rate finder for model performance, cost, and latency tuning.
nodes.norm_label: hyperparameter optimization and learning rate finder

nodes.label: Docker Image Registry Deployment
nodes.rationale: Docker images must be built and pushed to DockerHub, ECR, or GHCR.

nodes.label: CI/CD for ML Deployment
nodes.rationale: Automated deployments demonstrate continuous integration and deployment in an ML context.

nodes.label: TIMM or Hugging Face Model Choice
nodes.rationale: Students choose a classification model from TIMM or Hugging Face.
nodes.norm_label: timm or hugging face model choice
